name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
سَلْ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ كَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍۭ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَمَن يُبَدِّلْ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (٢١١)
```

```english
Ask the Children of Israel how many a manifest sign We had given them. Whoever changes Allah’s blessing after it has come to him, indeed Allah is severe in retribution. (211)
```

```arabic
زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۘ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (٢١٢)
```

```english
Worldly life has been glamorized for the faithless, and they ridicule the faithful. But those who are Godwary shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection, and Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning. (212)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** **سَلْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ كَمْ آتَيْنَاهُم مِّنْ آيَةٍ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَمَن يُبَدِّلْ نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ** — "Ask the Children of Israel how many a manifest sign We had given them. Whoever changes Allah’s blessing after it has come to him, then Allah is indeed severe in retribution." (2:211). This is a confirmation and reinforcement of what was contained in His saying, the Exalted: "And if you should lapse after the manifest proofs have come to you, then know that Allah is All-mighty, All-wise" (2:209)—the verse containing the threat of seizing the dissenters with the seizing of One who is All-mighty and All-powerful.

He says: These are the Children of Israel before your very eyes; they are the nation to whom Allah gave the Book, the judgment, prophethood, and kingship, provided them with good things, and favored them over all the worlds (العالمين). Ask them: how many a manifest sign (آية بينة) did We give them? Look at their affair: where did they begin and what was their ultimate fate? They distorted the words from their contexts and set up matters of their own making in opposition to Allah, His Book, and His signs, out of rebellious envy (بغياً) after knowledge had come to them. Consequently, Allah punished them with the severest punishment due to what befell them of taking peers (أنداد) besides Him, falling into discord, the splintering of opinions, devouring one another, the loss of sovereignty, the vanishing of felicity, and the torment of humiliation and misery in this world—and the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgraceful, and they shall not be helped.

This is the established sunnah (السنة الجارية) — *the habitual and consistent way of Allah’s acting in creation* — of Allah, the Glorified: whoever changes a blessing (نعمة) and diverts it from its proper course, Allah punishes him, and Allah is severe in retribution (شديد العقاب). Accordingly, His saying: "Whoever changes Allah’s blessing..." until the end of the verse, is an instance of placing a universal (الكلي) principle in the stead of a particular (الجزئي) case to indicate that the ruling is an established sunnah.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا وَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۘ وَالَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ** — "Worldly life has been glamorized for the faithless, and they ridicule the faithful. But those who are God-conscious shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection, and Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning." (2:212). This serves as the rationale (التعليل) for what preceded. The underlying factor in that [deviation] is the glamorization of the worldly life (تزين الحياة الدنيا) for them. For when life is glamorized for a human being, it invites him toward the whims of the soul (هوى النفس) and its desires, causing him to forget every truth and reality (حقيقة). Thus, the human being desires nothing but to attain it—whether through prestige, status, wealth, or finery. He soon begins to employ everything for its sake and in its path, including religion (الدين). He takes religion as a means (وسيلة) to achieve distinctions and social standing. Consequently, religion is transformed into a means for the distinction of leaders and chiefs and whatever suits their sovereignty and leadership, and a means for the commoners and blind followers (المقلدة) to seek proximity to their leaders and masters, as we witness in our nation today and as we witnessed in the Children of Israel before.

The apparent meaning of disbelief (الكفر) in the Quran is "concealing" (الستر), whether it be disbelief in the technical sense (كفراً اصطلاحياً) or absolute disbelief (كفراً مطلقاً) as opposed to absolute faith (الايمان المطلق). Thus, the glamorization of the worldly life is not restricted to disbelievers in the technical sense; rather, anyone who conceals a religious reality among the realities or changes a religious blessing is a disbeliever (كافر) for whom the worldly life has been glamorized—so let him prepare for a severe retribution.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "But those who are God-conscious (اتقوا) shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection..." The substitution of faith (الايمان) with godwariness (التقوى) — *the protective state of being conscious of Allah's boundaries* — in this sentence is because faith alone does not benefit without righteous deeds (العمل).
